sourceMovie.title: Pulse
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Pulse for the same reason it broke through in 2001: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Kiyoshi Kurosawa keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Polaroid
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pulse clicked for you, Polaroid (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pulse?
faq.answer: Try Polaroid, Haunter, Dust Devil—each shares DNA with Pulse while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pulse?
faq.answer: Pulse (2001) is best described as Horror, Thriller, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pulse worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
